hawkish_phrases: domestic cost pressures were strengthening and broadening
high levels of capacity utilisation and increasing labour market tightness
uncertainty about the outlook appeared to be receding
Longer-term inflation expectations seemed on the whole to be reasonably well-anchored
dovish_phrases: reduce, as of October, the monthly pace of the net asset purchases to €15 billion until the end of December 2018
end its net purchases at the end of December 2018
significant monetary policy stimulus was still needed
measures of underlying inflation remained generally muted
Growth projections for 2018 and 2019 had been revised down slightly
summary: The Governing Council unanimously decided to hold rates unchanged and confirmed the reduction of monthly asset purchases from €30 billion to €15 billion starting in October, with net purchases anticipated to end in December 2018, while maintaining dovish forward guidance that rates would remain at present levels at least through summer 2019, reflecting continued easing despite emerging cost pressures.
